headlines.title: What to Expect From Exelon's Q2 2026 Earnings Report
headlines.summary: Exelon will release its second-quarter earnings later this month, and analysts anticipate a double-digit bottom-line growth.

headlines.title: ComEd Commissions Two New Substations, Unlocking Up To 550MW of Wind Energy to the Grid
headlines.summary: CHICAGO, July 09, 2026--ComEd today announced the successful energization of two new 345 kV transmission substations to its grid, enabling the interconnection of up to 550 megawatts (MW) of wind generation in LaSalle and Woodford counties.

headlines.title: Exelon, FirstEnergy utilities’ RTO adder targeted by Maryland in FERC complaint
headlines.summary: Maryland state agencies asked the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to end an extra 0.5% return the transmission owners earn for being PJM Interconnection members.

headlines.title: Exelon to Announce Second Quarter Results on July 30
headlines.summary: CHICAGO, July 06, 2026--Exelon will hold its second quarter 2026 earnings conference call at 9:00 a.m. CT / 10:00 a.m. ET on Thursday, July 30, 2026. The conference call will be led by Exelon President and CEO, Calvin Butler, and Exelon Executive Vice President and CFO, Jeanne Jones.

headlines.title: ComEd’s Massive Storm Response Might Change The Case For Investing In Exelon (EXC)
headlines.summary: In early July 2026, Exelon’s ComEd unit spent several days restoring power to roughly 297,000 northern Illinois customers after multiple severe storms and extreme heat stressed the grid and prompted temporary conservation requests. The scale and visibility of these restoration efforts underscored Exelon’s role as essential infrastructure in managing both weather-driven disruptions and rising grid demand.

description: Exelon Corporation, a utility services holding company, engages in the energy distribution and transmission businesses in the United States. The company is involved in the purchase and regulated retail sale of electricity and natural gas; transmission and distribution of electricity; and distribution of natural gas to retail customers. It serves residential, commercial, industrial, and public authorities and electric railroads customers. Exelon Corporation was incorporated in 1999 and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.
